ludeoUESDKPlugin.description: Unreal Engine wrapper plugin for the Ludeo SDK
ludeoUESDKPlugin.repoNote: DEFINITIVE source for the Unreal plugin + bundled C SDK (release assets). Tags: https://github.com/ludeo-labs/unreal-plugin-releases/tags . Supersedes the legacy EdgeGamingGG/ludeosdk-unreal-plugin repo — use ludeo-labs/unreal-plugin-releases for all new acquisitions.
ludeoUESDKPlugin.privateNote: PUBLIC release repo — anonymous download works (no auth, no gh required). Transitional: if an anonymous download returns 404, the public flip is still pending — fall back to authenticated `gh` (`gh auth status`) or a browser download while logged in.

release.acquireLatest: REQUIRED FIRST STEP (unless the human pinned a version): resolve the latest release FROM THE REPO at download time. Do NOT trust the version numbers in this file or recalled from memory — they are an authoring-time snapshot and go stale; grabbing a pinned/old tag is the known failure this guards against. With gh (preferred): `gh release download -R ludeo-labs/unreal-plugin-releases -p '*.zip'` — omitting the tag downloads the LATEST. To see/record the tag first: `gh release list -R ludeo-labs/unreal-plugin-releases -L 1` (or `gh release view -R ludeo-labs/unreal-plugin-releases --json tagName`). Without gh: GET `https://api.github.com/repos/ludeo-labs/unreal-plugin-releases/releases/latest` and read `.tag_name` / `.assets[].browser_download_url`. Record the resolved tag in integration.json → sdkSetup.tag.
release.latestTagAtAuthoringNote: SNAPSHOT as of 2026-06 — may be stale. Do NOT download this verbatim; resolve the real latest per `acquireLatest`. Pin to a specific tag ONLY when the human explicitly asks for one.
release.ghDownloadNote: NO tag = the LATEST release — the default, recommended path (works on the public repo with NO auth; also works for the legacy private window if authenticated). Append a tag (`gh release download <tag> -R ...`) ONLY to honor a human-pinned version; confirm the tag first with `gh release list -R ludeo-labs/unreal-plugin-releases -L 1`.
release.noGhDownload: curl -L -O https://github.com/ludeo-labs/unreal-plugin-releases/releases/download/<tag>/LudeoUESDK-<tag>.zip   # resolve <tag> from the releases/latest API FIRST — see noGhDownloadNote; never hardcode a version
release.noGhDownloadNote: Use when `gh` is NOT installed — the repo is public, so a plain HTTPS GET works (curl/wget/Invoke-WebRequest, no auth). Resolve <tag> WITHOUT hardcoding a version: GET `https://api.github.com/repos/ludeo-labs/unreal-plugin-releases/releases/latest` and read `.tag_name` (and/or `.assets[].browser_download_url` for the `LudeoUESDK-<tag>.zip` URL directly), then download that. A human can also open `latestReleaseUrl` in a browser. (If anonymous GET 404s, see `privateNote` — the public flip may be pending.)
release.downloadUrlNote: Direct asset URL TEMPLATE — substitute <tag> with the tag resolved per `acquireLatest` (or the human-pinned tag). Do NOT invent a version; <tag> must come from the repo. On the public repo this works with anonymous tooling (curl/wget) and the browser alike — no auth needed.
release.bundledCSdkVersion: matches the plugin tag (e.g. plugin 4.3.1 bundled CoreSDK 4.2.2)
release.note: The release asset is FULLY self-contained: plugin root (.uplugin) + all 3 modules (LudeoSDK, LudeoUESDK, LudeoUESDKEditor) + the C SDK already populated at Source/LudeoSDK/SDK/ with binaries for Win64, Linux, and Android. The plugin release bundles its matching CoreSDK. Extract directly into Plugins/LudeoUESDK and the C SDK step is satisfied in the same download — no separate ludeoCSdk acquisition needed. Prefer this release asset over a GitHub source-archive zip, which would not resolve any LFS-tracked binaries. ALWAYS resolve the latest tag per `acquireLatest` (the asset name follows `LudeoUESDK-<tag>.zip`); pin to a specific tag only when the human specifies a version.
release.signatureNote: This config governs ACQUISITION (which release to download), not API signatures. Do not assume call signatures from the version number — grep the actual SDK headers under Source/LudeoSDK/SDK/ after extraction (the skill's Phase 0/2 guidance does this).

ludeoUESDKPlugin.note: Do not flag engine version compatibility as a risk — the plugin supports UE 4.27 through 5.7. Two acquisition paths: (a) release zip (see `release` above) — single self-contained download, the right choice for Perforce/no-submodule workflows and for git 'download & include'; (b) git submodule of the plugin repo, with the C SDK as a nested submodule (see ludeoCSdk).

ludeoCSdk.description: Native Ludeo C SDK — required by the UE plugin at Source/LudeoSDK/SDK/
ludeoCSdk.repoNote: Standalone C SDK releases (private). Latest tag v4.2.2, asset `LudeoCoreSDK_v<ver>.zip`. Supersedes the legacy EdgeGamingGG/ludeo-sdk-releases. PREFER the bundled SDK in the Unreal plugin release over this — only use this repo for the legacy git-submodule path.
ludeoCSdk.note: USE THE BUNDLED C SDK. It ships inside the LudeoUESDK release zip (plugin 4.3.1 bundles CoreSDK 4.2.2) — already populated at Source/LudeoSDK/SDK/, so the release-zip path needs NO separate C SDK acquisition. The standalone repo (ludeo-labs/sdk-releases) does publish a `LudeoCoreSDK_v<ver>.zip` asset (latest v4.2.2), but acquire it separately ONLY for the legacy git-submodule path (add as a nested submodule into Source/LudeoSDK/SDK). The bundled path via ludeo-labs/unreal-plugin-releases is the definitive source.
